target_game.name: Paper.io 2
target_game.core_loop: Draw trails to capture territory, expand your zone, and eliminate opponents by crossing their paths.
target_game.audience_proxy: Broad casual audience, appealing to both male and female players across a wide age range (10-35) looking for quick, competitive bursts.
target_game.visual_style: Vibrant, minimalist 3D top-down with bright, solid colors.

palette.description: Bright, high-contrast primary colors with neon accents.

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · in-game

centroid_hook.summary: Rapid block placement clearing a green grid.
centroid_hook.visual_action: Blocks are placed quickly into a grid, filling it up while a progress bar moves from 'SMART' to 'GENIUS'.

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 2d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "Rapid block placement clearing a green grid."

top_archetypes.label: Other · animation

centroid_hook.visual_action: Goku is depicted charging a blue energy blast against a vibrant, rainbow-colored background with glowing orbs.
centroid_hook.text_overlay: Dragon Ball Z Dokkan Battle PLAY NOW!

top_archetypes.rationale: 1 creatives across 1 unique advertisers, average age 620d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "Static character art of Goku Ultra Instinct with a prominent 'PLAY NOW!' call to"

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · animation

centroid_hook.summary: Goku SSJ3 performs his iconic Dragon Fist attack.
centroid_hook.visual_action: Goku charges energy and punches forward, summoning a massive golden dragon.

top_archetypes.rationale: 2 creatives across 2 unique advertisers, average age 367d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "Goku SSJ3 performs his iconic Dragon Fist attack."

top_archetypes.label: Satisfaction · mixed

centroid_hook.summary: A hand uses a stylus to solve a bus-sorting puzzle while the narrator asks a question about relaxation.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A hand with a stylus slides colorful buses around a crowded parking lot to clear a path for passengers.
centroid_hook.text_overlay: Hey! Played a game that Actually makes you Feel relaxed?
centroid_hook.voiceover_transcript: Hey! Have you ever played a game that actually makes you feel relaxed?

top_archetypes.rationale: 2 creatives across 2 unique advertisers, average age 114d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "A hand uses a stylus to solve a bus-sorting puzzle while the narrator asks a que"

top_archetypes.label: Curiosity · animation

centroid_hook.summary: Revealing secret underground bunkers beneath famous landmarks like the Eiffel Tower.
centroid_hook.visual_action: A cross-section view pans down from the Eiffel Tower to show a complex multi-story underground shelter.

top_archetypes.rationale: 3 creatives across 3 unique advertisers, average age 238d (velocity source: sov). Hook representative: "Revealing secret underground bunkers beneath famous landmarks like the Eiffel To"

game_fit_scores.rationale: The "SMART/GENIUS/PSYCHOPATH" satisfaction meter is a proven casual-arcade hook that maps cleanly onto Paper.io 2's broad audience and territory-fill payoff — swap the grid for the player's expanding colored zone and the meter fills as % captured. Frictions: the centroid palette (forest green + royal blue) clashes with Paper.io 2's high-saturation neon brand, and the source hook is block-placement (Tetris/block-blast adjacency), not trail-drawing, so we'd need to re-shoot in-engine rather than reuse assets. Worth a test, but only as a custom production — do not license or directly clone the source creative.

Palette overlaps incidentally (cyan/magenta/yellow neons), but the anime-IP static splash with energy-blast hero is fundamentally off-brand for a minimalist top-down .io game — there's no way to honestly represent trail-drawing or territory capture through Goku key art. Audience also skews to mid-core anime/RPG fans (Dokkan players), not the broad casual snackable-arcade crowd Paper.io 2 monetizes. Do not adapt; if the team wants a "character hero" angle, build a custom mascot/skin showcase instead of borrowing this archetype.

This is licensed Dragon Ball Z anime IP — using it for Paper.io 2 is a hard non-starter due to copyright/trademark exposure, regardless of creative performance. Beyond the IP issue, a charged-energy anime punch has zero mechanical link to drawing trails and capturing territory, and the dark indigo/purple palette clashes with Paper.io's bright minimalist look (only the gold overlaps). Skip this archetype; if the underlying signal is "satisfying power-release moment," test a safe analog like a massive territory-sweep finisher with a gold-burst payoff instead.

This is a classic puzzle/sort "relaxation" hook (bus jam / stylus ASMR genre) that has almost zero overlap with Paper.io 2's competitive territory-capture loop — showing a calm sorting puzzle would misrepresent the product and drive low-intent installs that churn fast. Palette (muted blue/purple/orange) and the "relaxed" emotional pitch directly conflict with the game's energetic, high-contrast neon visual identity and competitive .io tension. Audience demographics partially overlap (broad casual), but mechanic and tonal mismatch make this a poor fit; only worth testing as a misleading-style hybrid hook that pivots into actual Paper.io gameplay after 3s, and even then expect IPM but ROAS risk.

The dark, earthy palette (navy, burnt orange, forest green) and cross-section landmark reveal aesthetic clash hard with Paper.io 2's bright, flat top-down minimalist look — there's no natural way to render territory-capture gameplay as an underground bunker reveal without abandoning the brand. Mechanically the curiosity-reveal hook doesn't map to drawing trails or zone capture, so any adaptation would be a bait-and-switch that risks low IPM quality and install-to-play drop-off. Audience overlap on broad casual curiosity content is the only redeeming axis; recommend skipping unless the team wants to test a heavily reskinned "what's hidden under the map" reveal that pivots into actual gameplay within 2 seconds.

brief.title: SMART → GENIUS → PSYCHOPATH: Territory Fill Meter
brief.hook_3s: A neon-yellow zone explodes outward across the map as a meter rockets from SMART to GENIUS — can you push it to PSYCHOPATH?
scene_flow: 0-3s: Top-down Paper.io 2 arena, player's small yellow territory in a sea of grey. Trail loops out and SLAMS shut — a huge chunk floods yellow. Meter pops up: 'SMART' lights up.
3-7s: Faster captures, two cyan and magenta opponents get cut off and eliminated via kill-feed. Territory % climbs 22% → 54%. Meter advances to 'GENIUS' with a satisfying chime.

7-12s: Camera zooms slightly as the player carves an absurdly long, risky loop around the entire remaining map, dodging an enemy trail by one pixel. Loop closes — screen floods yellow. Meter pings 'PSYCHOPATH' in glitching magenta.
12-15s: '99% MAP CAPTURED' stamp, coins/gems rain into HUD, CTA card slams in over the fully-yellow board.
brief.visual_direction: Authentic Paper.io 2 top-down camera, minimalist 3D paper-thin arena. Player territory in primary #FFD700 gold, rival zones in #00BFFF cyan and #FF00FF magenta on a soft off-white grid. Native HUD intact: top-left % progress bar, kill-feed ticker top-right, coin/gem counters. Meter overlay sits bottom-center as a clean horizontal bar with three stops, glowing in the palette accents. Motion is snappy, every loop-close triggers a quick screen-flash and bloom — no extra characters, no stock arcade tropes, just the territory expanding and meter climbing.
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Paper.io 2 rotation is all unlabeled Unity playables and videos with no satisfaction-meter hook in market — this brings the trending SMART/GENIUS/PSYCHOPATH escalation framework that's spiking in casual UA but is underrepresented on this title. UA should test this against the existing Applovin playable as a video-first IPM lift play; the meter overlay is a cheap, modular asset we can A/B with different final-stage labels (PSYCHOPATH vs GODLIKE vs CHEATER).
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, authentic Paper.io 2 in-game top-down view: a vibrant minimalist 3D arena with bright solid colors, player's gold #FFD700 territory occupying ~70% of a soft off-white paper grid, thin cyan #00BFFF and magenta #FF00FF rival zones being cut off, a glowing yellow trail mid-loop about to close a massive capture, native Paper.io 2 HUD visible (top-left territory percentage progress bar reading 70%, kill-feed text top-right, coin and gem counters), bottom-center horizontal three-stage meter overlay with the middle stop 'GENIUS' lit in neon magenta, no extra characters, crisp bloom on trail edges, high-contrast neon palette. [Audio: VO (gleeful, rising) 'Oh, that's GENIUS' / punchy upbeat trap 120BPM with rising synth riser / whoosh on trail draw, satisfying chime + bass thump on loop-close at 2.5s.]

Storyboard frame 1, aspect 9:16, Paper.io 2 top-down arena early game: small gold #FFD700 player territory in lower third of a pale grid, two opponent zones in cyan #00BFFF and magenta #FF00FF, a short yellow trail extending outward, vibrant minimalist 3D with bright solid colors, native HUD (progress bar at 18%, kill-feed empty, coin counter), bottom-center meter overlay with first stop 'SMART' just lighting up in gold, clean energetic competitive mood, no characters added. [Audio: VO (calm, curious) 'Start small...' / minimal trap kick 110BPM building / soft pen-on-paper SFX for trail, light ding when SMART lights at 1s.]

Storyboard frame 2, aspect 9:16, Paper.io 2 climactic top-down shot: arena nearly entirely flooded gold #FFD700, last sliver of cyan #00BFFF rival being eliminated, magenta #FF00FF accent glow around the screen edge, native HUD shows 99% progress bar, kill-feed text stacked top-right, coin/gem rain animation, vibrant minimalist 3D bright solid colors, bottom-center meter overlay fully maxed with 'PSYCHOPATH' stamped in glitching neon magenta, screen-flash bloom, no added characters. [Audio: VO (unhinged shout) 'PSYCHOPATH!' / hard-hitting trap drop 128BPM with 808s / screen-flash whoosh, coin cascade chimes, deep bass impact at 0.8s.]

brief.title: "Relaxing? Think Again." — Satisfaction Bait-&-Switch for Paper.io 2
brief.hook_3s: A finger calmly traces a perfect neon-yellow square across a pastel grid — "oddly satisfying" ASMR vibe — until a magenta enemy slices the trail and the screen EXPLODES into Paper.io 2 chaos.
scene_flow: 0–3s: Top-down close-up of a fingertip slowly drawing a clean #FFD700 trail on a soft grid, ASMR pen-on-paper SFX, narrator asks the satisfaction question.
3–6s: Camera pulls back — the 'puzzle' is actually a Paper.io 2 arena. A magenta opponent races toward the unfinished trail. Tension spike.
6–10s: Player frantically swipes to close the loop, captures a huge territory chunk in a #FFD700 flash, kill-feed pops 'YOU ELIMINATED Pink_42'.
10–13s: Territory % bar rockets from 12% → 68%, coins/gems scatter, screen shakes with combo chimes.
13–15s: End card: Paper.io 2 logo on neon grid, CTA button pulses.
brief.visual_direction: Lock to Paper.io 2's authentic minimalist 3D top-down camera with bright solid color blocks. Open soft and pastel to mimic the trending "relaxation" archetype, then snap to the game's signature high-contrast palette: #FFD700 player territory, #00BFFF rival zone, #FF00FF danger trails. Keep the grid floor, thin trail extrusion, and HUD chrome (% bar top-left, kill feed top-right, coin counter) pixel-faithful to the live game. No invented characters — the player is represented by the standard Paper.io 2 square avatar only.
text_overlays: Ever played a game that ACTUALLY relaxes you?
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Paper.io 2 rotation is all straight gameplay video + playables with no copy — they're missing the satisfaction/ASMR bait-and-switch hook that's winning in the broader puzzle-sort market. This brief borrows the trending "relaxation question" opener for top-of-funnel scroll-stop, then pivots hard into authentic competitive .io gameplay by 3s to protect intent and ROAS. UA team: test against the 12/23 Admob video as control; expect higher IPM, monitor D1 retention to confirm the pivot filters out pure-puzzle seekers.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, aspect 9:16, authentic Paper.io 2 in-game moment: minimalist 3D top-down view of a bright #FFD700 yellow square avatar mid-draw, extruding a thin glowing yellow trail across a soft pastel grid floor, a #FF00FF magenta rival square approaching from the right edge on a collision course, #00BFFF cyan captured territory visible in the background, faithful Paper.io 2 HUD with territory percentage bar top-left and kill-feed text top-right, vibrant minimalist 3D top-down with bright solid colors, on-screen text overlay 'Ever played a game that ACTUALLY relaxes you?' in bold white sans-serif. [Audio: VO (calm female): 'Ever played a game that actually relaxes you?' / soft lo-fi ASMR pads 70 BPM building tension / pen-on-paper scratch SFX, faint heartbeat at 2.5s]

Storyboard frame, aspect 9:16, authentic Paper.io 2 gameplay: dramatic top-down zoom of the #FFD700 yellow trail snapping closed into a massive captured territory flood-fill, #FF00FF magenta enemy eliminated mid-screen, scattered collectible coins and gems bouncing, kill-feed text 'YOU ELIMINATED Pink_42' top-right, territory bar surging to 68%, #00BFFF cyan rival zones shrinking, vibrant minimalist 3D top-down with bright solid colors palette #FFD700 #00BFFF #FF00FF, bold on-screen text overlay '68% TERRITORY 🔥'. [Audio: VO (excited): 'Wait — not so relaxing now!' / punchy upbeat electronic 128 BPM drop / whoosh on capture, coin chime stack, satisfying bass thud at 1.8s]

Storyboard end card, aspect 9:16, Paper.io 2 logo centered on a neon grid floor with #FFD700 #00BFFF #FF00FF territory tiles radiating outward, minimalist 3D top-down with bright solid colors, glowing 'Play Free' CTA button pulsing at bottom, faint % bar and coin HUD framing, on-screen text 'Can YOU survive?'. [Audio: VO (punchy): 'Can you survive?' / energetic competitive synth stinger 130 BPM / UI click on CTA, rising whoosh into logo reveal]

brief.title: What's Hidden Under the Map?
brief.hook_3s: "What's hidden UNDER the paper map?" — camera peels back a Paper.io 2 territory grid like a sticker, revealing a secret neon vault beneath.
scene_flow: 0-3s: Top-down Paper.io 2 match in progress — yellow player carving a trail through cyan/magenta territories. Finger taps the map. A glowing crack appears.
3-6s: Camera tilts into a cross-section: the flat paper-territory layer peels up to reveal a hidden multi-tier vault below stacked with gems, coins, and a giant '?% captured' progress bar.
6-10s: Player snaps back to top-down. Now racing to capture a massive uncharted zone, dodging two AI opponents closing in on the trail. Kill-feed pops: 'You eliminated Blue!'
10-13s: Bonus-level screen flashes — gems and coins rain in, territory meter rockets from 32% to 87%.
13-15s: End card: yellow territory dominates the screen, CTA pulses.
brief.visual_direction: Lock to Paper.io 2's signature vibrant minimalist 3D top-down: flat shaded territory blocks in #FFD700 yellow (hero), #00BFFF cyan (rival), #FF00FF magenta (rival), thick solid trail lines, no textures. The "reveal" beat briefly tilts the camera 30° to show the paper map as a literal thin layer with a hidden treasure vault below — same flat-color language, just stacked vertically — before snapping back to clean top-down. Keep HUD authentic: top-left territory % progress bar, right-side kill feed text, floating gem/coin pickups. Energetic, high-contrast, zero gradients.
brief.rationale: Voodoo's current Paper.io 2 rotation leans on straight gameplay capture and fail/retry playables — none use the curiosity-reveal hook that's trending across .io and casual scans. This brief imports the "what's hidden underneath" mechanic but reskins it natively to Paper.io 2's flat-paper metaphor (peel the map → secret vault), so it earns the curiosity click without bait-and-switch and pivots to real gameplay by 3s. UA: test against the Applovin playable as control; expect lift on CTR/IPM from cold casual audiences.
scenario_prompts: Hero frame, 9:16 vertical, authentic Paper.io 2 in-game moment: vibrant minimalist 3D top-down view, flat solid colors #FFD700 yellow player territory mid-screen with a thick yellow trail line extending into uncaptured white space, #00BFFF cyan and #FF00FF magenta rival territories on edges, a glowing crack splitting the paper map open to reveal a hidden lower layer with floating gems and coins beneath, top-left territory progress bar reading '32%', right-side kill-feed text, bold white sans-serif overlay 'What's UNDER the map?' centered top, no characters, bright high-contrast solid colors no gradients no textures. [Audio: VO (curious whisper) 'Wait... what's under the map?' / energetic competitive synth 124 BPM building tension / paper-peel rip SFX at 1s, low bass reveal hit at 2s, coin shimmer at 3s]

Storyboard frame 2, 9:16 vertical, authentic Paper.io 2 gameplay: top-down flat-shaded 3D arena, #FFD700 yellow player carving an aggressive trail loop around a huge uncaptured zone, two rival blobs in #00BFFF cyan and #FF00FF magenta closing in from opposite sides, scattered collectible gems and coins along the path, kill-feed text 'You eliminated Blue!' top-right, territory bar filling fast at '64%', bold overlay 'Capture it before they do' lower third, vibrant minimalist style solid colors no gradients. [Audio: VO 'Get it before they do!' / punchy upbeat competitive trap 130 BPM / whoosh on trail draw, chime on gem pickup at 2s, glass-shatter elimination SFX at 3.5s]

Storyboard frame 3 end card, 9:16 vertical, Paper.io 2 victory state top-down: screen dominated by #FFD700 yellow territory ~87%, tiny slivers of #00BFFF cyan and #FF00FF magenta cornered, bonus-level screen overlay with cascading coins and gems, progress bar maxed glowing, large bold overlay 'PLAY FREE' bottom center with pulsing arrow, vibrant minimalist 3D flat solid colors high contrast. [Audio: VO 'Your turn — play free' / triumphant energetic synth stinger 128 BPM / coin-rain cascade SFX, victory chime at 1s, button-tap click at 4s]
